dev_docs.title: Mouth Delta Logit Gap Probe

metric.name: Δmouth−Δcontext
metric.definition: Average difference between logit drop caused by masking the mouth region and masking an equally sized non-mouth context patch for Wearing_Lipstick, Smiling, and Mouth_Slightly_Open.
metric.formula: Δmouth−Δcontext = (1/N) * Σ_i [(z_i^clean − z_i^mouth) − (z_i^clean − z_i^context)] where z are logits for the targeted attribute.

dev_docs.expectation: A well-trained model should rely on mouth evidence, so Δmouth−Δcontext ≥ 0.15 indicates stronger causal dependence on relevant pixels than on background cues.

threshold.comparison_logic: If metric ≥ threshold ⇒ probe passes; otherwise it signals spurious reliance on context.

dev_docs.data_source: Training/validation mini-batches already loaded during training (images, logits, mouth bounding boxes derived from facial landmarks).
data_processing_steps: Run a forward pass on each batch to obtain clean logits for the three mouth-centric attributes.
Apply binary occlusion to the mouth box and to a random non-mouth patch, recompute logits, and accumulate the Δmouth−Δcontext difference per sample before averaging.
dev_docs.interpretation_logic: Positive margins show mouth pixels drive predictions; negative or small margins imply contextual shortcuts and failing Lips/Smile detection.
dev_docs.visualization_plan: Call wrapper_plot.create_bar_plot to display attribute-wise Δmouth−Δcontext bars plus a horizontal line for the 0.15 threshold.

json_output_example.expectation: Mouth occlusion should reduce logits at least 0.15 more than context occlusion.

dev_docs.title: Mouth Flip Rate Specificity Probe

metric.name: FlipSpecificity
metric.definition: Ratio between the probability of a prediction flipping when the mouth is occluded and the probability of flipping when a non-mouth region is occluded.
metric.formula: FlipSpecificity = P(pred flips | mouth mask) / P(pred flips | non-mouth mask), computed over samples where sigmoid(z) > 0.5 before occlusion.

dev_docs.expectation: For a good model, mouth occlusion should cause at least 60% more state flips than other regions (ratio ≥ 1.6).

threshold.comparison_logic: If ratio ≥ 1.6 ⇒ probe passes; smaller ratios suggest predictions ignore causal lip evidence.

dev_docs.data_source: Training-time inference batches with access to predicted labels and logits per attribute.
data_processing_steps: Record binary predictions for target attributes, apply mouth occlusion and random non-mouth occlusion separately, and count flip events per case.
Compute empirical probabilities of flips for both occlusions and form their ratio.
dev_docs.interpretation_logic: High ratios confirm mouth sensitivity specifically triggers decisions, while low ratios mean the model flips on irrelevant regions equally or more often.
dev_docs.visualization_plan: Use wrapper_plot.create_histogram for flip-count distributions and wrapper_plot.create_line_plot to show FlipSpecificity over training epochs.

json_output_example.expectation: Mouth occlusion should flip predictions at least 1.6× more often than other regions.

dev_docs.title: Occlusion Strength Correlation Probe

metric.name: R_strength
metric.definition: Spearman correlation between occlusion strength (percentage of mouth pixels masked) and the corresponding drop in logits for the three mouth-centric attributes.
metric.formula: R_strength = SpearmanCorr({s_k}, {Δz_k}), where s_k is the occlusion strength level and Δz_k is the resulting logit drop aggregated over samples.

dev_docs.expectation: A causal model should show monotonic decline with stronger mouth masking, so correlation should exceed 0.7.

threshold.comparison_logic: If correlation ≥ 0.7 ⇒ probe passes; otherwise, responses to incremental occlusion are inconsistent.

dev_docs.data_source: Saved logits at multiple occlusion strengths applied during training batches (e.g., mask 25%, 50%, 75%, 100% of mouth region).
data_processing_steps: Generate graded occlusion masks for each batch, run forward passes to capture logits per strength level, and compute Δz per level.
Aggregate Δz across batches and compute Spearman correlation between occlusion strengths and mean Δz.
dev_docs.interpretation_logic: Strong positive correlation confirms the model’s confidence decreases smoothly as more lip evidence is removed; weak correlation signals noisy, non-causal reliance.
dev_docs.visualization_plan: Plot average Δz vs occlusion strength using wrapper_plot.create_line_plot and overlay correlation value in the title.

json_output_example.expectation: Logit drop should increase monotonically with occlusion strength, yielding correlation ≥ 0.7.
